Title: Identification of propranolol and derivatives that are chemical inhibitors of phosphatidate phosphatase as potential broad-spectrum fungicides

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: P131/Guy11/70-15/ZB25 strains used in study; source genotype for purified protein not explicitly specified
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phosphatidate phosphatase (Lipin family); Lipin_N and LNS2 domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phosphatidic acid to diacylglycerol conversion branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Phosphatidic acid (PA); NBD-PA used in enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dephosphorylates phosphatidic acid to produce diacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal lipid homeostasis, development, appressorium formation, and pathogenic infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MoPAH1 activity promotes normal lipid metabolism and fungal virulence; chemical inhibition of MoPah1 reduces DAG-forming activity, alters PA/DAG-related lipid balance, and suppresses growth and infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with purified GST-MoPah1 and LC-MS; molecular docking; lipidomics; RNA-seq/qRT-PCR; comparison with Mopah1 mutant sensitivity; plant infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MoPah1 directly catalyzes conversion of PA to DAG in vitro. 2. Propranolol inhibited MoPah1 enzyme activity by about 60% at 0.2 mM. 3. Chemical inhibition of MoPah1 altered lipid metabolism, impaired lipid/glycogen utilization during appressorium formation, and reduced fungal growth and pathogenicity. 4. Derivative II-8 bound directly to MoPah1 and showed stronger antifungal activity than propranolol.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in mycelia cultured in liquid minimal medium; downstream lipid-metabolism phenotypes evident during appressorium formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Propranolol treatment; derivative II-8 treatment; appressorium induction on hydrophobic surfaces; field/greenhouse infection conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported for lipid homeostasis and virulence, but explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Chemical inhibition causes strong suppression of fungal development and pathogenicity; off-target binding of derivatives remains possible
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MoPah1 is a promising fungicide target for broad-spectrum disease control; structure-guided inhibitor optimization such as propranolol derivative II-8 may improve efficacy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potential propranolol-interacting residues predicted as Ser24 and Gly479. Inhibition was attenuated in the Mopah1 mutant. Human and some oomycete homologs contain an additional Lipin_mid domain, potentially affecting inhibitor sensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA896260; China National Genomics Data Center PRJCA019319; lipidomics by HPLC-MS
